queue.title: Fix checkout completion: 39.4% vs 45-50% benchmark (+$9-17K/mo)

effort.description: Checkout audit: express pay, trust, errors, abandonment flow

queue.rationale:
894 shoppers/28d begin checkout; only 352 finish (39.4%) vs a 45-50% DTC benchmark. Every point of completion is ~$1.6K/mo: reaching 45% = +$8.9K/mo, 50% = +$16.8K/mo. This is the single largest concrete leak in the funnel — shoppers at this stage already chose the product.

Evidence: begin_checkout=894/28d | purchases=352 | completion=39.4% | benchmark=45-50%
Dashboard impact: $8,884/mo estimated at the 45% target

execution_brief.action: Audit and fix the checkout → purchase drop-off (60% of started checkouts abandon)
execution_brief.summary: Shoppers who reach checkout already want the product. Work the standard completion levers in order of effort, measuring weekly.

steps.title: Express payment prominence
steps.before: Verify: are Shop Pay / Apple Pay / Google Pay shown FIRST at checkout on mobile?
steps.after: Express wallets above the fold on step one. Shop Pay alone typically completes ~1.7x better than manual card entry.

steps.title: Kill surprise costs
steps.before: Shipping cost first revealed at checkout — the #1 stated abandonment reason.
steps.after: Show shipping cost/threshold on PDP and cart ('Free US shipping over $X'); consider folding cost into price if margin allows.

steps.title: Check for technical errors
steps.before: Unknown error rate on payment step (EU cards, address validation).
steps.after: Segment completion by country and device in GA4; investigate any segment completing <30%.

steps.title: Abandoned checkout recovery
steps.before: Verify the abandoned-checkout email is enabled and firing (ties into sprint #164).
steps.after: 1h + 24h recovery emails with the cart contents; no discount in the first email.

out_of_scope: No theme redesign; checkout-scoped changes only this sprint.
execution_brief.definition_of_done: Completion rate measured weekly; +8% relative lift over the 39.4% baseline within 28 days.

queue.title: Stand up lifecycle email: post-purchase + winback flows

effort.description: Email flows: welcome, post-purchase onboarding, winback

queue.rationale:
Only 4.1% of 1,134 customers ever buy again (DTC pet benchmark: repeat = 15-25% of revenue). At the current ~$62K/mo run-rate, repeat orders contribute ~$2.5K/mo (4%) vs ~$9.3K/mo at a conservative 15% benchmark — a ~$6.8K/mo steady-state gap. Buyers who DO return come back fast (median 8 days), so week-one flows capture most of it. Email-attributed orders today: ~2/28d. The ladder is proven by co-purchase data: harness -> leash -> carrier ($286-LTV cohort) -> Bootcamp (14% repeat).

Evidence: repeat_rate=4.1% | repeat_rev_share=4% vs 15% benchmark | median_days_to_2nd=8 | email_orders_28d~2
Dashboard impact: $6,800/mo steady-state estimated (ramps over ~2 quarters as cohorts stack)

execution_brief.action: Build 3 email flows in Klaviyo (or Shopify Email): welcome, post-purchase, winback
execution_brief.summary: The store acquires ~95 new customers/month and loses 96% of them after one order. Three automated flows recover the proven ladder: harness buyers → leash (160 manual co-purchases/yr), carrier buyers → travel kit, everyone → Bootcamp.

steps.title: Post-purchase onboarding (fires day 2-7)
steps.before: No post-purchase flow. Median 2nd purchase happens at day 8 — unsupported.
steps.after: Day 2: setup/fit guide for the product bought (cuts returns). Day 5: cross-sell the proven pair — Houdini buyers get Kushma leash, Zeppelin buyers get Go Litter. Day 12: Cat Bootcamp intro (its buyers repeat at 14%).

steps.title: Welcome flow for non-buyers
steps.before: Signup incentive exists (WELCOMEBACK code used 31×/yr) but no structured flow.
steps.after: 3 emails: brand story + best-seller guide + first-purchase nudge. Personalize by landing page category (training blog reader vs product browser).

steps.title: Winback (fires day 45)
steps.before: No winback. 96% of customers never hear from the store again.
steps.after: Day 45: 'How's the harness working out?' + review request + ladder offer. Non-discount first; discount variant only if no purchase by day 60.

out_of_scope: No list buying. No daily campaigns — flows only.
Deliverability check (SPF/DKIM/DMARC) before ramping volume.
execution_brief.definition_of_done: Three flows live; email-attributed orders visible in Shopify order attribution within 30 days.

queue.title: Scale Meta budget on Zeppelin (reported ROAS 4-9 vs 3.3x breakeven)

effort.description: Raise budget on the working campaign; watch marginal ROAS

queue.rationale:
Operator-reported Meta ROAS on Zeppelin is 4-9x vs a 3.3x breakeven at 30% gross margin — already profitable, likely under-funded. Zeppelin sessions are worth $2.37 each ($4.2 counting the cohort's $286 LTV vs $216 first order). Scaling paid sessions +2,300/mo at even ROAS 4 adds ~$5.5K/mo revenue (~$1.4K net margin after spend); at reported mid-range ROAS the net roughly doubles. Now that ads carry UTMs, Lisa measures the GA4 side of this weekly.

Evidence: reported_ROAS=4-9 | breakeven_ROAS=3.3 | zeppelin_rps=$2.37 | LTV_multiple=1.32x
Dashboard impact: $5,500/mo revenue estimated at conservative ROAS

execution_brief.action: Raise Meta budget stepwise on the Zeppelin campaign while marginal ROAS holds above ~4
execution_brief.summary: Your own reporting says this prints money. Scale in 20-30% weekly increments, not one big jump, and watch frequency + CPM.

steps.title: Raise budget +25%
steps.before: Current budget (operator knows); ROAS 4-9 reported.
steps.after: +25% budget. Hold 5-7 days. If marginal ROAS stays >=4, repeat. Stop scaling when it approaches 4.

steps.title: Watch fatigue signals
steps.before: Single creative risks fatigue at higher spend.
steps.after: If frequency >3 or CPM climbs >30%, add 1-2 creative variants before the next raise.

steps.title: Verify with Lisa's numbers
steps.before: Platform ROAS flatters itself with view-through attribution.
steps.after: Cross-check: paid sessions to /products/zeppelin-cat-carrier x $2.37/session should roughly cover spend x 3.3 at breakeven.

out_of_scope: No new campaigns or audiences this sprint — scale what works.
execution_brief.definition_of_done: Zeppelin sessions +30% with GA4-side paid revenue per session holding above $1.85.

queue.title: A/B test #2 — Zeppelin PDP hero: travel-usp framing (ATC)

kpi.display_label: Guardrail: page CR · primary metric = ATC in A/B app

effort.description: Set up in Essential A/B Testing; 42-day pre-registered run

queue.rationale:
Zeppelin converts views to cart at 7.0% vs Houdini's 14.6% — the weakest ATC among the money PDPs, on a $190 product. Detectable: ±23% ATC in 4wk, ±17% in 8 — so this tests a BOLD swing only. Hypothesis: buyers hesitate because fit/airline anxiety isn't answered above the fold. Variant B leads the hero with 'Fits under airline seats' + head-hole comfort framing + kit savings.

PRE-REGISTERED PROTOCOL: primary metric = add-to-cart rate; horizon = 42 days; no early stop; ship at >=95% AND horizon AND clean guardrails. NOTE: run AFTER sprint #174 ships the always-on airline section (SEO content must not be inside an A/B variant — Google must always see it).

Dashboard impact: $1,300/mo estimated (+20% ATC x ~60% pass-through on ~$10.9K/mo page revenue)

execution_brief.action: Run a 42-day hero-copy A/B on the Zeppelin PDP after #174's airline section is live
execution_brief.summary: Bold swing only — the traffic supports detecting ±17-23% ATC changes, not tweaks.

steps.title: Ship #174 first (sequencing)
steps.before: Airline-fit section not yet on the page.
steps.after: Airline section live for ALL visitors (SEO needs one consistent version). The A/B tests the hero copy only.

steps.title: Create the test
steps.before: Hero leads with 'ZEPPELIN™ Cat Carrier With Head Hole'.
steps.after: Variant B: 'The Airline-Ready Carrier Cats Actually Like' + subline 'Head-hole design keeps them calm · fits under the seat · save $30 in the Travel Kit'.

steps.title: Pre-register, decide at day 42, log
steps.after: Same protocol as test #1; learning logged regardless of outcome.

out_of_scope: Not concurrent with any other Zeppelin test; ads (#171) may run — randomization keeps arms comparable.
execution_brief.definition_of_done: Concluded at horizon, logged, winner live.

queue.rationale:

Evidence: 'cat harness' 16.5K imp @ pos 12.3 | 'escape proof' variants 12.7K imp @ pos 4.7-5.4 | page CR 3.0%

execution_brief.summary: The escape-proof positioning won its niche; now claim the category head term without losing it.

steps.title: H1 + title carry the head term

steps.after: SEO title: 'Cat Harness — Escape-Proof Houdini™ | Vet-Trainer Designed'. Keep 'escape proof' in H1; add a 'Why this cat harness' section using the bare term naturally 4-6x.

steps.title: Internal anchors say 'cat harness'

steps.after: Switch 3-5 high-authority blog links to exact/partial anchors: 'cat harness', 'escape-proof cat harness'.

steps.after: Add 4-question FAQ (sizing, safety, kitten fit, leash pairing) — targets People-Also-Ask real estate.

queue.title: Mobile PDP test: lift view→add-to-cart (11.3% vs 16.7% desktop)

effort.description: Mobile PDP: sticky ATC, above-fold price/CTA, gallery weight

queue.rationale:
Mobile gets 4× desktop's product views (16.3K vs 3.9K/28d) but converts views to cart at 11.3% vs 16.7%. Honest caveat: overall mobile session-CR (1.34%) already beats desktop (1.15%), so this is a test, not a broken funnel — casual mobile browsing may explain part of the gap. Ship sticky add-to-cart + above-fold price/CTA and judge on measured ATC rate, not vibes.

Evidence: mobile_view_atc=11.3% | desktop_view_atc=16.7% | mobile_views_28d=16290 | mobile_session_cr=1.34%
Dashboard impact: $1100/mo estimated

execution_brief.action: Ship 3 mobile PDP changes, measure view→ATC for 28 days
execution_brief.summary: Highest-traffic surface in the store. Three standard mobile fixes, one measurable KPI.

steps.title: Sticky add-to-cart bar on mobile PDP
steps.before: CTA scrolls away under gallery + description.
steps.after: Price + Add-to-cart pinned at bottom of viewport once the main CTA scrolls out.

steps.title: Tighten above-the-fold
steps.before: Gallery dominates first screen; price/variant/CTA below fold on small screens.
steps.after: First viewport shows: product name, price, review stars, variant selector, CTA.

steps.title: Check pop-up & chat interference on mobile
steps.before: Unknown — audit whether popups cover the CTA on small screens.
steps.after: Popups delayed ≥30s and never covering the ATC; chat bubble above the sticky bar.

out_of_scope: No checkout changes in this sprint.
No desktop changes — mobile template only.
execution_brief.definition_of_done: All 3 live on mobile; ATC rate read at day 28 vs 12.4% baseline.

queue.title: A/B test #1 — Houdini PDP headline: benefit-led vs feature-led (ATC)

kpi.display_label: Guardrail: page CR · primary metric = ATC in A/B app

effort.description: Set up in Essential A/B Testing; 28-day pre-registered run

queue.rationale:
First loop iteration on the highest-power surface: Houdini PDP gets 9,049 product views/28d at 14.6% add-to-cart — enough traffic to detect a ±14% ATC change in 4 weeks (±10% in 8). Hypothesis: the above-fold copy leads with the product name/feature; a benefit-led headline plus a one-line trust row (509 buyers last year, trainer-designed, escape-proof guarantee) raises ATC.

PRE-REGISTERED PROTOCOL: primary metric = add-to-cart rate; horizon = 28 days (full weeks), no early stop; ship if >=95% probability AND horizon reached AND guardrails clean (AOV, refund rate, page CR). Log result to learnings/ab-tests.yml either way.

Dashboard impact: $950/mo estimated (+15% ATC x ~60% pass-through on ~$10.5K/mo page revenue)

execution_brief.action: Run a 28-day headline A/B on the Houdini PDP in Essential A/B Testing
execution_brief.summary: One variable, one metric, fixed horizon. The app randomizes and computes significance; Lisa keeps the ground-truth guardrails.

steps.title: Create the test in Essential A/B Testing
steps.location: Shopify admin → Apps → A/B Testing → New test → this product
steps.before: Above-fold: 'Houdini™ Escape Proof Cat Harness' + price + variants.
steps.after: Variant B headline: 'The Cat Harness They Can't Escape' + subline 'Trainer-designed · escape-proof · 500+ adventure cats geared up last year'. Nothing else changes.
steps.note: 50/50 split, all traffic. One variable only — no simultaneous price or image changes.

steps.title: Pre-register and calendar the decision
steps.before: No entry.
steps.after: Add the test entry with started date, baseline 14.6% ATC, horizon 28 days. Decision date goes in your calendar — results are read once, at the horizon.

steps.title: At day 28: decide and log
steps.after: >=95% prob & guardrails clean -> ship B store-wide, log WINNER + learning. Otherwise revert, log LOSER/INCONCLUSIVE + learning. Either way the ledger feeds Claude's future copy.

out_of_scope: No peeking-to-stop. No mid-test edits to either variant. No second concurrent test on this page.
execution_brief.definition_of_done: Test concluded at horizon, result logged in learnings/ab-tests.yml, winner live.

queue.title: Merchandise bundles: Adventure Kit + existing Zeppelin travel kit

effort.description: 1 bundle product + PDP cross-sell blocks on 2 pages

queue.rationale:
160 orders/yr manually combine Houdini harness ($29.99) + Kushma leash ($39.99) — 31% of Houdini buyers — yet no Adventure Kit bundle exists. Meanwhile the Zeppelin + Go Litter kit EXISTS ($199.99, saves $30) but barely sells while 69 buyers/yr assemble the pair manually at full price: a merchandising gap, not a product gap. 52% of all orders are single-unit.

Evidence: houdini_kushma_pairs=160/yr | zeppelin_golitter_pairs=69/yr | single_unit_orders=52% | AOV=$122.68
Dashboard impact: $319/mo estimated

execution_brief.action: Create the Adventure Kit bundle and surface both kits on their component PDPs
execution_brief.summary: Customers already buy these pairs at full price — package them, price with a modest save, and put them where the pair-buyers are deciding.

steps.title: Create 'Adventure Kit' bundle product
steps.location: Shopify → Products → Add product (or Shopify Bundles app)
steps.before: No Houdini + Kushma bundle. 160 orders/yr pair them manually at $69.98.
steps.after: Adventure Kit™ — Houdini harness + Kushma leash at $64.99 (save $5). Use existing photography of the pair in use.

steps.title: Add kit cross-sell block on both PDPs
steps.before: Houdini and Kushma PDPs don't mention each other or any kit.
steps.after: 'Complete the kit' block under add-to-cart: on Houdini show Adventure Kit; on Zeppelin show the existing Zeppelin + Go Litter kit ($199.99, save $30).

out_of_scope: No sitewide discounting.
No new photography required to launch.
execution_brief.definition_of_done: Adventure Kit purchasable; both PDPs show their kit; AOV tracked for 60 days.

steps.title: Rewrite first paragraph of product description
steps.before: (could not fetch — check Shopify)
steps.after:
Here's the current opening:

> *"Take your cat training to the next level with the help of our training kit! All the tools and guidance you need to get started, and the tips, tricks, and fun activities to help you teach your feline friend proper behaviors, cute tricks, and strengthen the bond between you two in the process."*

The #1 benefit buried at the end is the **bond** — which is the real emotional hook. Here's the rewrite:

---

**Option A (bond-first, warm):**
> The best thing clicker training does isn't teaching tricks — it's changing your relationship with your cat. This kit gives you everything to start: a quiet clicker, target stick, treats pouch, and a step-by-step guide built for real cats and real beginners.

---

**Option B (outcome-first, punchy):**
> A cat that actually listens — and a relationship you didn't know was possible. That's what clicker training unlocks. This starter kit has everything you need to get there: quiet clicker, target stick, treats pouch, and a beginner-friendly guide.

---

**Option C (problem-first, relatable):**
> Most cats aren't stubborn — they just haven't been taught the right way. Clicker training changes that, and this kit makes it easy to start today. Quiet clicker, target stick, treats pouch, and a step-by-step guide included.

---

My pick: **Option A** — it leads with the emotional truth (the bond), flows naturally, and doesn't sound like ad copy. Want me to push one of these live?
steps.note: Lead with the #1 benefit, not brand name. Make the first sentence scannable.

queue.title: Zeppelin: claim 'airline approved cat carrier' (pos 22-25 today)

effort.description: Airline-fit content section + schema + blog anchor links

queue.rationale:
Zeppelin ranks #1 for its brand terms but pos 22-25 for 'airline cat carrier' / 'airline approved cat carrier' / 'cat carrier for airplane' (~1,500 imp/90d visible, more latent). The page never says 'airline approved' or lists airline dimensions. Buyers searching these terms are the exact $190 customer, and the how-to-fly blog (queued #134) can funnel authority.

Evidence: 'airline cat carrier' 333 imp @22 | 'airline approved' 200 imp @25 | 'for airplane' 227 imp @13
Dashboard impact: $420/mo estimated (Zeppelin RPS $2.37, order value $216)

execution_brief.action: Add an airline-fit section to the Zeppelin PDP and wire airline-intent anchors to it
execution_brief.summary: The page has the authority (ranks #1 on brand); it just never says the words buyers search.

steps.title: 'Fits under the seat' section with airline table
steps.before: No mention of airline compliance, under-seat dimensions, or specific airlines.
steps.after: Add section: 'Airline-approved carry-on for cats' with a dimensions table vs Delta/United/AA/Southwest under-seat specs, TSA screening note, and in-cabin policy links.

steps.title: Anchor links from travel content
steps.before: Blogs link with brand anchors.
steps.after: Anchors: 'airline approved cat carrier', 'cat carrier for airplane travel' -> Zeppelin PDP.

out_of_scope: No claims of formal airline certification — phrase as 'meets under-seat dimensions for...'
execution_brief.definition_of_done: 'airline approved cat carrier' pos <=10 within 6 weeks; airline-query clicks visible in GSC.
